transcript.text: I don't know why every time I always join with these, the mic and the camera enabled, and then I get here and they're off, but here I am.
off for you?
Happy Friday.
Happy Friday.
How's the week been?
It's been good.
It's been productive.

I feel like now that we're out of launch mode, we've just spent the whole week going back and grabbing the things, everything that got dropped, sort of, and moving forward on other things, which is a really good feeling.
It's kind of like when you finally clean your house after you've had, like, a really busy weekend, and then you're like, let me just, like, put this back together.
Like, that was good, but, like, yeah.
Yes, absolutely.
Absolutely.
I'm sorry to jump straight in.
Wanted to introduce Andi.
Yeah.
Hi, Deedi.
I know you guys are like halfway through the sprint.
I lead customer operations.
So Erik just said he was doing like a check-in call and I was like, well, I'd love to like, you know, listen in and hear how it's going.
Cool.
See?
Absolutely.
So how is it going?
Good.

So I wanted to basically like take a second to like make sure that you like see what you guys thought felt like the vibe was and then like five track on our side and then kind of look at the SoW and like sort of all of the items that we had added in there to make sure that we're sort of like on track for all of them.
I think on my side, I like want to start by like acknowledging that like we haven't been as available for you in the first four weeks as like we will be going forward.
Just because of the timing, but I think that worked out nicely because a lot of the work that your team was doing was creating the infrastructure for the rest of this engagement.
That said, I do feel like I'm missing often a feeling of strategy leadership from the team, I think.
So a couple of times I've had to specifically be like, okay, can we turn this scrunch PDF into a report with insights?
Or can you guys tell us which queries we should be tracking?
And those are the kind of examples that is literally the reason why I hired you guys.
I feel like we could produce content with lots of different tools ourselves.
And like, I'm not saying that you all aren't doing a great job in like the content ops area because you definitely are.
But like that is replicable, I think, in other areas.
And the reason why I was really drawn to your business.
Model is the idea of like having this like strategic like agency to help us like set the stage and I feel like I've had to kind of like pull to get that so far.
So I think that's like my main, my main vibe, Jack, because that's the piece of it that I'm most looking to you guys for.
Yeah, understood.

And I kind of had the feeling that was the case, mainly because I think we did get briefed on kind of what was in the SoW, but haven't really maintained like a clear view on what's expected there from like a week to week basis and kind of, you know, which prompts should we be pulling into track.

And so that we can definitely take kind of on our side is, you know, we definitely need to improve both the just proactive manner in which we're kind of reviewing the SoW so that we can make sure that we meet all those requirements.

And we are hopefully kind of going to turn the corner here, I know, some of the more kind of strategic, you know, where should we be going, what should we kind of be tracking, is definitely, hopefully going to come through in this competitive brief we're putting together, which is 75% of the way done, we're just waiting for the SERP to AI migration kind of data poll and analysis.
So we will have that for you.
I'm hoping by end of today, if not early Monday.
But yes, I do hear you on that.

And I do want to make sure that we kind of are getting in front of it, because it does feel like we could be a little bit more proactive in kind of the strategic levers that we're helping you guys pull, especially as we get into more of the geo conversations and kind of where do we want to go from where we are today.
Yeah, totally.
Yeah, I like, I mean, I have four, four direct reports, and none of them are specifically dedicated.

You know, maybe like, I mean, Sarah does, you know, a lot of just editorial ops, but like, yeah, I would, I think I would love for like, conversations to start with a recommendation, like more often, be like, we did the research and like, here's what we think makes the most sense, like given our expertise, and then like, have us like kind of discuss those like paths forward.
And like, I don't know what the rest of like these, these like next four weeks, like typically look like either, obviously, so I'm like, take, take that with a grain of salt.
Yeah, absolutely.

Yeah, I think it's a little different with every client going through the strategy sprint process, but we definitely are, you know, kind of like you said, setting up the infrastructure, both from kind of our operational side and getting kind of all of our artifacts refined, making sure we're on brand and voice.
And then I think we spent a little more time on getting those right, because of the product changes that were happening almost real time.
Very important.
Yeah, absolutely.
Especially with the new competitor.
And kind of positioning that we're changing, it was definitely a priority to make sure that we get those right so that the content that we do produce is reflecting the kind of most up-to-date.
So I do think we over-indexed a bit there as a team when we could have probably spent a little bit more time on the strategy pieces.
But I think going forward, we definitely hear you and can make sure that we come with, you know, kind of our own recommendations.
So we do writing guidelines, like bottom line up front, here's the recommendation that we're leading with, and here's why.
And then we can get into kind of what the content updates are.
Yeah.
I think I just wanted to like also just make sure that, because I know that the SoW was like structured like a little bit more like first four weeks, second four weeks.
And I just wanted to make sure that we didn't think we were done with all the things that were kind of listed in those first four weeks.
And like, I'm totally fine with it moving around because we needed to make sure we had the product stuff right, which like genuinely is the most important part.
So like, I'm not upset about that.
But yeah, as long as we like are still working on those.
Other pieces of it, then I feel a little bit better.
Yeah, absolutely.
And I think that your reminder last week, when you pinged me about it, I was just like, oh, yeah.
Yeah, I mean, I sent Tatiana that PDF of Scrunch, and she was like, this isn't the report, is it?
And I was like, no.
Let me get back to you.
Yeah, and it was lacking the comprehensive prompt that we needed.
So, but I think we are in a much better place now.
We have a much better view on kind of visibility.
We split out the cohorts and did the analysis on both of them to kind of see kind of, are you guys maintaining that strong foothold in no code?
And kind of, where are you at as long as the AI app dev kind of cohort?
So, got plenty of stuff to share there, as well as just kind of overall meta-analysis of like what we've seen for content and formatting analysis.
So, very eager to share that with you as soon as it's ready.
I think other...
Pieces that we are really focused on kind of in this strategy sprint process is making sure that we can get up to the velocity that we've agreed to like the five per week.
And so I think that's where Ada and our content managers have spent the majority of their time.
And so I think going forward, you'll see me more involved with kind of the strategy pieces and let them stay maintained and focused on kind of getting content right.
So definitely heard on all fronts there.
Cool.
Yeah.
Awesome.
Yeah.
And then I think just like the last thing I would note that like I I feel like I'm like eagerly waiting for is the piece of under the content roadmap bucket in the SLW.
That's about like sort of that those other pieces and other channels.
So we had listed strategic guidance for the marketing site, like the forum and the manual high level best practices for like Reddit, YouTube, social.
So.
Like, so that we can start to, on my side, take some actions on the other surfaces that y'all are not, like, creating content for.
Like, to have that sort of, like, 360-degree GEO strategy.
Like, you know, where, like, do we need to add FAQ sections to every single page on our marketing site, right?
Like, those kinds of questions, like, I am also responsible for.
And so that's, like, the other piece of this that I am really, like, looking for from you guys.
Like, not the creation of those FAQs necessarily, right?

But, like, hey, you should be doing these things across all these other surfaces that you're responsible for in order to, like, help the content that you guys are actually creating, like, go even further.
Yeah, absolutely.
Cool.
Yeah, I think from that standpoint, some of the findings that we have from the analysis that we're doing right now will definitely help guide that directionally.
And then we can kind of see where.
We need to go a little bit deeper on each of the specific line items.
I think the forum and the manual definitely need to have another kind of review outside of kind of our audit that we've done just on the main site in the blog.
So we can definitely kind of prioritize getting a team member to help us focus there.
Yeah.
I'm curious about the forum because it's like Reddit to like, just to put that out there, like to help direct this a little bit.
Like we actually, the forum like gets crawled pretty well.
We like every month there's like some random forum post that like blew up that's like totally unrelated to like our actual product.
Right.
And so, so we often actually like when we look internally at these metrics, we like exclude forum URLs because it's a lot of noise.
But with, with Reddit and like those kinds of websites being prioritized more and more, I think my question around the forum is like, should we be using it in this?

Same way that we like start to use Reddit, like for that reason, like, is it going to get treated by these LLMs more like Reddit than like our blog or is it going to get treated more like our blog than like Reddit and like, and whatnot.
And then the manual, the manual is like the main source of, that's our docs basically, right?
Like that's what our LLMs are ingesting for product guidance.
So like when someone's asked ChatGPT how to do something on Bubble, they're like mainly reading our manual.
So that's just like a little bit of context behind those two surfaces and why they're interesting to me.
Yeah, absolutely.
I've actually spent some time in the forum just because they are being cited.
Yeah.
was going through this scrunch data.
It was interesting.
was like, or even in SEMrush, it's like ChatGPT versus Claude.
And I'm like, how is this showing up?
Is there a blog post?
And like, no, it's just a conversation in the forum that's getting, you know, 20 or so clicks per month.
Um, yeah, definitely interesting.
So I'm.
Related.

Sometimes, like, user apps that, like, do something really crazy or, like, not safe for work, like, will pop up and, like, or, like, you know, like, like, a book that someone has, like, kind of, like, sourced.
Oh, The Art of Letting Go.
Yeah, you know what I mean?
Like, it's, every month there's, like, something crazy in the forum that pops up in our, in our trends.
That was the one that stood out, that's The Art of Letting Go book.
Oh, yes, that's it.
Receives a lot of traffic.
I know.
I sent a, I sent a note to our legal team.
I was, like, can we make them take this down?
And she said, we're actually, like, we can't do that until the publisher of the book reaches out to us.
I know.
Interesting.
I'm, like, so I'm a book person, so I was, like, this is theft.
But, alas.
Awesome.
Well, we definitely now have the SoW in front of the working team, so we will be sure to, kind of, keep track.
And make sure that we hit all those.
Thanks.
Obviously, we're a little behind on kind of the week-by-week one, but we'll want to make sure that we get those before we transition to kind of Phase 2 or assuming we transition to Phase 2.
So we'll make sure to have those as a priority for the next few weeks going forward.
Yeah, that sounds good.
That sounds good.
I'm just, I just like, I'm really looking to feel like 200% confident at the end of the eight weeks that like the strategic partnership is what we need and not just like the content production.
Yep, definitely.
Cool beans?
Cool beans.
I picked that up from a manager that I had during an internship in college and I have never let it go.
Everywhere I've worked, people have uploaded cool beans emojis because of me.
It's one of my favorite little Slack responses, this little cool bean guy.
little cool beans, yeah.
Yeah.
Cool, awesome.
Anything else top of mind?
Um, I...
I don't think so.
That sounds good.
We're looking forward to reading through the content.
Absolutely.
Yeah.
Wonderful.
Any big plans this weekend?
Yeah, my husband and I are, so we have a almost 16 month old.
And so we have independently been away from home, but we have not gone somewhere away from home together, just the two of us.
So my mom's coming to stay with her this weekend, and we're just going to like a little casino spa resort in the Catskills for the weekend.
We're going to get a couple's massage.
We're going to hang out in the sauna.
I'm really excited about it.
I'm sure my husband will drag me to the blackjack tables.
We're going to go to the steakhouse.
It's going to be like, just, I'm really excited to just, I love that baby, but man, life is different when your weekends are just as exhausting.
Right.
Oh my goodness.
I can't imagine.
I have no kids.
I
Six nieces and nephews, but I get to play the fun uncle and kind of just freeze in, have fun, and then be like, okay.
Yeah, exactly.
Like, it's so funny.
Like, at the end of, like, a three-day weekend, I'm like, who are you?
Like, I'm tired.
Like, 6 p.m.
on the end of a three-day weekend, I'm like, you're going to bed.
Like, we all need you to go to bed.
But, yeah, no, it's really fun, but it's definitely different.
Like, Monday mornings, I log in.
Like, she goes to daycare, and I'm like, can I not just sit on the couch for, like, the first half of the day?
Like, I just need to, like, but yeah.
She's just in that mode where she's, like, starting to, like, not even just walk everywhere, but run.
Like, she's, like.
Oh, yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
But it's fun.
It's a very fun face.
She's super cute.
Do are you doing anything fun this weekend?
Uh, no.
I'm going to try to keep it that way.
I've had things planned the last few weekends, and so just going to have a nice, casual, laid-back weekend.
Probably just, yeah, couch riding and watching football.
Amazing.
What's your
Team?
Chiefs and Iowa.
Okay.
I was a Chiefs fan before we were good.
That's fair.
My husband, I mean, I live in Syracuse, New York.
So as you can imagine, like, I'm like deep in Bill's country.
And like, I married into like, I mean, they have like, my husband had season tickets when we lived in Jersey City.
Like, and that's like eight hours for Buffalo.
Like, deep in Bill's.
Deep, I married deep into the Bill's mafia.
Yeah.
Josh Allen is my fantasy quarterback.
So I've at least got that connection with you.
There want to know something very fun.
So obviously one of our, one of our CEOs name is Josh.
And we, for a long time, had a VP of product whose name was Allen, spelled the same.
And so people would tag like at Josh at Allen, and then like say something.
And every time I was like, ha ha.
Look at it.
Awesome.
Andy, don't know if you have any thoughts.
No, this was really helpful.

I can help just make sure that like also helpful just thinking about what you're looking for post sprint and how we would set that up and making sure that there's still that strategic alignment throughout the partnership because I think we think about it as kind of like strategic checkpoints, but definitely can like be more proactive.
So it's helpful to think about that from a resourcing standpoint to make sure that you guys get what you need throughout.
Cool Beans, again.
Awesome.
Great.
Well, enjoy the weekend and we will check with you next week.
Sounds good.
Back at you.
All right.
Thanks, Deedi.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Check-in on sprint progress and align on strategic expectations.](https://fathom.video/share/4-yKVM1ydHptYUzSByuHeLkprJ82nx5V?tab=summary&timestamp=91.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Strategic Gap:** Bubble needs proactive strategic leadership, not just content ops. GrowthX must shift from reactive fulfillment to leading with recommendations to justify the partnership's value.](https://fathom.video/share/4-yKVM1ydHptYUzSByuHeLkprJ82nx5V?tab=summary&timestamp=132.0)
  - [**SoW Reset:** The team must reset to the Statement of Work (SoW), which was deprioritized for urgent product changes. All Phase 1 deliverables must be completed before Phase 2.](https://fathom.video/share/4-yKVM1ydHptYUzSByuHeLkprJ82nx5V?tab=summary&timestamp=394.0)
  - [**Competitive Brief:** A key deliverable, the competitive brief, is 75% complete and will be shared by early next week. It will analyze Bubble's standing in both the no-code and AI app dev markets.](https://fathom.video/share/4-yKVM1ydHptYUzSByuHeLkprJ82nx5V?tab=summary&timestamp=243.0)
  - [**Channel Strategy:** GrowthX must provide guidance for other surfaces (marketing site, forum, manual) to enable a 360-degree SEO strategy and maximize content impact.](https://fathom.video/share/4-yKVM1ydHptYUzSByuHeLkprJ82nx5V?tab=summary&timestamp=516.0)

## Topics

### Strategic Expectations vs. Delivery

  - [Bubble's core need is proactive strategic leadership, not just content production.](https://fathom.video/share/4-yKVM1ydHptYUzSByuHeLkprJ82nx5V?tab=summary&timestamp=132.0)
  - [The partnership's value is currently unclear, as Bubble has had to pull for strategic insights (e.g., "turn this PDF into a report with insights," "tell us which queries to track").](https://fathom.video/share/4-yKVM1ydHptYUzSByuHeLkprJ82nx5V?tab=summary&timestamp=132.0)
  - [GrowthX acknowledged this gap, attributing it to over-indexing on operational setup and real-time product changes.](https://fathom.video/share/4-yKVM1ydHptYUzSByuHeLkprJ82nx5V?tab=summary&timestamp=330.0)
  - [**Path Forward:** GrowthX will adopt a "bottom line up front" approach, leading with recommendations and their rationale before discussing content updates.](https://fathom.video/share/4-yKVM1ydHptYUzSByuHeLkprJ82nx5V?tab=summary&timestamp=377.0)

### SoW Alignment & Deliverables

  - [The SoW was deprioritized due to urgent product changes and a new competitor.](https://fathom.video/share/4-yKVM1ydHptYUzSByuHeLkprJ82nx5V?tab=summary&timestamp=330.0)
  - [**Action:** GrowthX will reset to the SoW, ensuring all Phase 1 deliverables are completed before transitioning to Phase 2.](https://fathom.video/share/4-yKVM1ydHptYUzSByuHeLkprJ82nx5V?tab=summary&timestamp=394.0)
  - [**Competitive Brief:** This key deliverable is 75% complete.](https://fathom.video/share/4-yKVM1ydHptYUzSByuHeLkprJ82nx5V?tab=summary&timestamp=243.0)
      - [**Blocker:** Awaiting SERP to AI migration data.](https://fathom.video/share/4-yKVM1ydHptYUzSByuHeLkprJ82nx5V?tab=summary&timestamp=243.0)
      - [**ETA:** End of day Friday or early Monday.](https://fathom.video/share/4-yKVM1ydHptYUzSByuHeLkprJ82nx5V?tab=summary&timestamp=243.0)
      - [**Analysis:** Compares Bubble's standing in the no-code vs. AI app dev cohorts.](https://fathom.video/share/4-yKVM1ydHptYUzSByuHeLkprJ82nx5V?tab=summary&timestamp=449.0)

### Guidance for Other Channels

  - [Bubble needs strategic guidance for other surfaces to enable a 360-degree SEO strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/4-yKVM1ydHptYUzSByuHeLkprJ82nx5V?tab=summary&timestamp=516.0)
  - [**Forum:** Gets crawled well but creates "noise" in metrics.](https://fathom.video/share/4-yKVM1ydHptYUzSByuHeLkprJ82nx5V?tab=summary&timestamp=621.0)
      - [**Question:** Should it be treated like Reddit (prioritized by LLMs) or a blog?](https://fathom.video/share/4-yKVM1ydHptYUzSByuHeLkprJ82nx5V?tab=summary&timestamp=621.0)
  - [**Manual (Docs):** The primary source for LLMs providing product guidance.](https://fathom.video/share/4-yKVM1ydHptYUzSByuHeLkprJ82nx5V?tab=summary&timestamp=673.0)
      - [**Question:** How can it be optimized for this use case?](https://fathom.video/share/4-yKVM1ydHptYUzSByuHeLkprJ82nx5V?tab=summary&timestamp=673.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/4-yKVM1ydHptYUzSByuHeLkprJ82nx5V?tab=summary&timestamp=200.0)
      - [Deliver the competitive brief by end of day Friday or early Monday.](https://fathom.video/share/4-yKVM1ydHptYUzSByuHeLkprJ82nx5V?tab=summary&timestamp=243.0)
      - [Adopt a "bottom line up front" approach for all future recommendations.](https://fathom.video/share/4-yKVM1ydHptYUzSByuHeLkprJ82nx5V?tab=summary&timestamp=377.0)
      - [Prioritize completing all Phase 1 SoW deliverables.](https://fathom.video/share/4-yKVM1ydHptYUzSByuHeLkprJ82nx5V?tab=summary&timestamp=394.0)
      - [Develop strategic guidance for the marketing site, forum, and manual.](https://fathom.video/share/4-yKVM1ydHptYUzSByuHeLkprJ82nx5V?tab=summary&timestamp=516.0)

action_items.description: Deliver competitive brief w/ SERP-to-AI migration data to Deedi
Assign team member to review Bubble forum + manual; then deliver guidance to Deedi
